summary: This notebook demonstrates a pipeline for converting text descriptions into SVG illustrations optimized for a specific competition metric. It leverages Stable Diffusion to generate initial bitmaps, then applies a custom contour extraction and adaptive simplification algorithm to convert them into vector graphics. By strategically injecting an OCR decoy and evaluating outputs against a composite metric of VQA fidelity, aesthetic quality, and OCR detection, the author shows that post-processing generation outputs can significantly boost metric scores. Readers should learn how to bridge generative image models with vector graphics for metric-specific optimization and understand the trade-offs between SVG byte limits and visual fidelity.
competition_flow: Loads text descriptions from CSV/Parquet, generates bitmaps via Stable Diffusion, converts them to SVGs using contour extraction and adaptive simplification, adds OCR decoys, evaluates with a custom metric, and outputs the highest-scoring SVG per description.
data_reading: Parses nested JSON fields into lists/dicts
Merges parsed question data with train_df on id
data_processing: Applies JPEG compression (quality=90) during metric evaluation
ensembling: Generates 3 candidate bitmaps per description, converts each to SVG, evaluates all against the custom metric, and selects the highest-scoring output.
insights: Converting SD-generated bitmaps to SVGs with contour-based extraction preserves metric-relevant structure better than raw raster outputs.
Injecting a strategically placed OCR decoy reliably boosts OCR scores without harming aesthetic or VQA metrics.
Adaptive polygon simplification and color quantization are critical for staying within SVG byte limits while maintaining visual fidelity.
